steps.title: Identify doubling condition
steps.content: We want f(t+T) = 2 f(t). Substitute the expression: $$80000·2^{(t+T)/790} = 2·80000·2^{t/790}$$ . Cancel 80000 and divide both sides by $2^{t/790}$: $$2^{(t+T)/790} = 2·2^{t/790} = 2^{1 + t/790}$$ . Equate exponents: $$\frac{t+T}{790} = 1 + \frac{t}{790}$$ .

steps.title: Solve for T
steps.content: Subtract $\frac{t}{790}$ from both sides: $$\frac{T}{790} = 1$$ . Multiply by 790: $$T = 790$$ minutes. That matches choice B — B is correct.
